world.title: New Opening: Mababe Tented Camp by Bush Ways Botswana
world.description: Bush Ways Botswana is proud to announce the opening of Mababe Tented Camp, its newest safari property located in the wildlife-rich Mababe Concession a key corridor linking the renowned areas of Khwai, Savuti and Chobe.

world.title: backed group’s hacking of Director Kash Patel’s personal email: The information is ...
world.description: Tech News News: The FBI has officially acknowledged that Iranian-linked hackers targeted the personal email account of its director, Kash Patel, confirming the breach.

world.title: Russian man sentenced for UK attack witnessed by Barron Trump
world.description: A London court has sentenced a Russian man to four years in prison for assaulting a woman during an attack that Barron Trump witnessed on a video call.

world.title: Pro-Iranian hacking group claims credit for hack of FBI Director Kash Patel's personal account
world.description: A pro-Iranian hacking group claimed Friday to have hacked an account of FBI Director Kash Patel and has posted online what appear to be years-old photographs of him, along with a work resume and other personal documents. Many of those records appeared to be more than a decade old.

world.title: Nato allies increase defence spending by 20% for 2025
world.description: Nato’s European members and Canada raised defence spending by 20% in real terms for 2025 compared with the previous year, according to Secretary General Mark Rutte’s annual report on 26 March 2026.

world.title: Can America and Iran Reach a Cease-Fire?
world.description: Both sides want to dictate the terms—but neither truly can.

world.title: 9 Under-The-Radar Malaysian Islands To Consider Instead Of Thailand Or Indonesia
world.description: Lesser-known Malaysian islands such as Pulau Sibu, the Perhentian Islands, and Pulau Penang offer travelers amazing snorkeling, sand, and island getaways.

world.title: Israeli Travelers Keep On Getting Detained in Malaysia During Transits Because They Relied On AI to Check it Was Safe
world.description: Young Israeli travelers keep getting detained in Malaysia during layovers at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KUL) simply because of their nationality, it has been alleged, and apparently, it’s because they all relied on AI assistants to check whether it was safe to travel through Malaysia. If they had instead checked official travel information provided by…

business.title: US Stocks Fall; S&P 500 Set for Longest Losing Streak Since ‘22
business.description: US stocks slumped, heading for their longest stretch of losses in four years, as the escalating hostilities in Iran deepened concern that higher oil prices will cripple the global economy.

business.title: European Commission investigates cyber attack on its websites
business.description: The Commission said the attack was “contained.”

business.title: The Damang gold mine: Ghana’s strategic asset at the centre of ownership controversy
business.description: Explore the history, strategic value, and current ownership dispute of the Damang Gold Mine in Ghana, including the government takeover and Engineers and Planners’ bid. Read more.

business.title: Meet Claude Mythos: Leaked Anthropic post reveals the powerful upcoming model
business.description: An internal leak at Anthropic has revealed company information including its new most powerful model known as Claude Mythos.

business.title: U.S. stocks fall, oil prices rise as Trump's delays fail to quell fears
business.description: The S&P 500, Nasdaq and Dow Jones Industrial Average all sank in early trading, a day after Wall Street's worst drop since the conflict with Iran started. Canada's main stock index was in positive territory, however.

business.title: Braskem to appeal Brazil's AD ruling on US PE: CEO
business.description: Petrochemical giant Braskem will appeal the Brazilian government's Thursday decision setting definitive antidumping duties of $199/t on imports of US polyethylene (PE) and $238/t on Canadian grades, chief executive Roberto Ramos said.

business.title: SCE Extends More Than 1,000 Offers to Community Members for Eaton Fire Recovery
business.description: Southern California Edison today announced a major milestone in the Wildfire Recovery Compensation Program, with more than 1,000 compensation offers extended...

business.title: Independent petrol stations close pumps as cost hits £2 a litre
business.description: Rather than face customer abuse and allegations of profiteering, small retailers are choosing to shut their forecourts as wholesale prices rise

science.title: Trade-offs between commercial and public satellite data in water mapping accuracy revealed
science.description: A new study finds that commercial satellite imagery data often outperforms public data sets when identifying surface water, but that public data sets may be better at detecting water hidden by forest cover. Satellite imagery is a powerful tool for mapping surface water, from the movement of rivers and streams to water levels and even water temperatures. The effectiveness of those satellites depends on their ability to identify water in the images they capture.

science.title: Meet NASA’s Artemis II crew, who will usher in a new era of deep-space exploration
science.description: The crew of the Artemis II mission will include the first woman and person of color to travel to deep space, ushering in a new era of representation in exploration beyond Earth.

science.title: He suddenly couldn't speak in space. NASA astronaut says his medical scare remains a mystery
science.description: The astronaut who prompted NASA's first medical evacuation earlier this year said Friday that doctors still don't know why he suddenly fell sick at the International Space Station.

science.title: He suddenly couldn't speak in space. NASA astronaut says his medical scare remains unsolved
science.description: The astronaut who prompted NASA’s first medical evacuation earlier this year said Friday that doctors still don’t know why he suddenly fell sick at the International Space Station.

science.title: AI Links Brain Rhythms to Physical "Wiring" Across Lifespan
science.description: How does brain activity change from childhood to old age? Researchers introduce Xi–αNET, an AI model linking EEG rhythms to nerve-signal speed and myelination. Discover why alpha waves slow down as we age.

science.title: JWST solves decades-long mystery about why Saturn appears to change its spin
science.description: Researchers at Northumbria University have used the most powerful space telescope ever built to answer one of the longest-standing puzzles in planetary science—why does Saturn appear to spin at a different speed depending on how you measure it? The findings, published in the Journal of Geophysical Research: Space Physics, reveal for the first time the complex patterns of heat and electrically charged particles in Saturn's aurora, and show that the entire system is driven by a self-sustaining feedback loop powered by the planet's own northern lights.

science.title: Ignition Sets a Challenge the Space Industry Must Meet
science.description:
By Steve Altemus 
NASA’s “Ignition” announcement, as presented by Administrator Jared Isaacman this week, outlined an unquestionably bold vision to accelerate the United States’ return to the Moon in a sustainable way. This vision puts us, as an industry, on footing that defies the impossible. The timelines for delivery are incredibly short, and the technical hurdles appear insurmountable. However,  Imagine what we can achieve and how far we will go if we accept and embrace NASA’s challenge. Th

science.title: Why audiences matter for honeybee waggle dance success
science.description: Honey bees are known for their famous “waggle dance” - which is a figure-of-eight routine that tells their hive mates exactly where to find food. But just like on Strictly Come Dancing, not every performance is the same. Scientists have noticed that even when the same bee is dancing about the same patch of flowers, the quality can vary. Why? ai.title: SA Asks: What's next for Super Micro Computer?
ai.description: Super Micro Computer's stock has plunged amid news its co-founder has been accused of smuggling AI tech into China. Seeking Alpha analysts discuss what's next for the company.

ai.title: AI Founders Are Chasing The Wrong Thing

ai.title: TRUMP AMERICA AI Act proposes sweeping changes to AI liability, Section 230 repeal, and federal oversight
ai.description: The TRUMP AMERICA AI Act seeks to fully repeal Section 230, eliminating legal protections for online platforms regarding user-generated content. This would force platforms into aggressive censorship to avoid lawsuits over controversial posts, chilling free speech and investigative journalism.
